content.full_text:
November 16, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Request for Advice, Conflict of Interest Question

My position is Assistant City Manager with the City of Moreno Valley. The position of Assistant City Manager has been made subject to the Political Reform Act by the City's conflict of interest code. My varied duties include administering certain contracts for the City.

I request your advice in regard to the following questions:

1) Is a potential conflict of interest created for me in carrying out my duties as the contract administrator if a contracting agency, which I administer, threatens to sue the City and myself personally regarding the manner with which I have administered the contract?

2) If the answer to 1) above is yes, would a potential conflict of interest exist if I believed that I was at virtually no risk in being held liable for my actions and if I believed that my future actions regarding the contract administration would not be influenced by the threat of a personal lawsuit?

3) If the answer to 1) is yes and 2) is yes, then is there any action I could take in order to be able to continue in my capacity as contract administrator?

Please direct your response to me, in care of City of Moreno Valley, P.O. Box 1440, Moreno Valley, CA 92556-1440. Since I am currently serving in the role as the contract administrator, your prompt reply and guidance on this important matter will be appreciated.

Sincerely,

Gene Rogers
Assistant City Manager

GR/lcs
92gr470

cc: Norman R. King, City Manager
     Ed Duddy, City Attorney
